subject: Sociology
title: Testing and Assessment Culture
description: A short lesson to help you apply Testing and Assessment Culture.

hook.text: A single test score decides college admission, scholarship money, and self-worth. That's a lot of pressure.

buildup.text: Standardized testing promises objective measurement. Every student takes the same test under the same rules.

discovery.text: But tests reflect cultural knowledge that varies by class. Questions assume experiences not all students share.

twist.text: Teaching to the test narrows learning. Schools focused on scores abandon creativity, curiosity, and critical thought.

climax.text: Assessment shapes what gets taught and who gets ahead. The test isn't neutral — it's a power tool.

punchline.text: Tests measure background as much as brains.

quiz.question: What is a key criticism of standardized testing?
options: They reflect cultural knowledge that varies by class
